eventMetadata.context_description: Prime Minister Robert Abela's snap election call on April 27, dissolving Parliament ahead of the May 30 vote amid Iran war tensions, has intensified focus on a tight Labour-Nationalist race, with polls averaging Labour at 51% and Nationalist at 45%, locking the top two spots nationally. Trader consensus favors AD+PD at 64.5% implied probability for third-highest vote share, reflecting its consistent 3-4% polling, established green platform, and historical edge over Momentum's variable 2-5% centrism. A non-binding April 17 coordination pact between AD+PD and Momentum aims to minimize vote splits via district candidate pacts, while recent candidate announcements and May Day rallies signal active third-party campaigns, though fragmented "others" remain marginal. Nominations close May 11.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Shanghai be 25°C on May 3?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 3 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Shanghai Pudong International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/cn/shanghai/ZSPD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus overwhelmingly favors 22°C (100%) as Shanghai's highest temperature on May 3, backed by real-time observations from the Shanghai Pudong International Airport station—the market's resolution source—peaking at 22°C amid persistent overcast skies and northerly winds under 12 km/h. China Meteorological Administration reports confirm this, with global models like GFS and ECMWF ensembles projecting identical highs due to limited solar insolation following May 2's moderate rain and a cool air mass. Compared to early May climatological averages of 23–25°C, cloudy conditions suppressed daytime heating. Realistic challenges include unexpected late-afternoon clearing allowing brief solar-driven spikes to 23°C, though low-level wind shear and model agreement make this improbable; final CMA validation expected soon.

The resolution source for this market will be information from NOAA, specifically the highest reading under the "Temp" column on the specified date once information is finalized for all hours on that date, available here: https://www.weather.gov/wrh/timeseries?site=LLBG

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the "Switch to Metric Units" button until the relevant table displays °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects the latest short-range forecast models projecting a high temperature of around 21°C at Ben Gurion International Airport near Tel Aviv on May 4, with 42% implied probability, closely trailed by 20°C and 22°C outcomes. This positioning stems from recent model runs, including BBC and statistical ensembles like world-weather.info, showing highs of 21°C amid partly cloudy skies, light rain showers, and fresh westerly breezes moderating after May 1's observed 24°C peak. Mediterranean sea breezes and northerly flow are key factors capping intensity below seasonal averages of 24-26°C. Uncertainty remains low at 48 hours out, but overnight updates from Israel Meteorological Service and NOAA could shift probabilities ahead of resolution based on official airport measurements.

markets.question: Will the Fed’s lower bound reach 0.5% or lower before 2027?
markets.description:
The FED interest rates are defined in this market by the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds range. The decisions on the target federal fund range are made by the Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings.

This market will resolve to “Yes” if the lower or the upper bound of the target federal funds rate reaches the specified level at any point by December 31, 2026, 12:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

Emergency rate cuts and hikes outside the regularly scheduled meetings will be considered.

The resolution source for this market is the official website of the Federal Reserve at:
https://www.federalreserve.gov/monetarypolicy/openmarket.htm.

This market may resolve as soon as the relevant data showing the reached level is published.

eventMetadata.context_description: The Federal Reserve held the federal funds target range steady at 3.50%-3.75% following its April 28-29, 2026 FOMC meeting—the third consecutive pause—amid accelerating inflation and a resilient labor market. March 2026 CPI rose 3.3% year-over-year, up sharply from February's 2.4%, while unemployment dipped to 4.3%, bolstering trader consensus for sustained restrictive policy to combat persistent price pressures. CME FedWatch Tool-implied probabilities show markets pricing minimal changes through year-end 2026, aligning with the March dot plot's median projection of around 3.00%-3.25% by end-2027. Key catalysts ahead include April CPI data on May 12 and the June 16-17 FOMC meeting, where hotter readings could reinforce hold or hike expectations.

markets.question: Will Doja Cat have a #1 song on the Billboard Hot 100 in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if any song by the listed artist is the number 1 song in any official weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs chart released for a data collection reference period entirely between January 1 and December 31, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Any song released with the listed artist as a primary artist will count, including songs released before 2026. If a song is released jointly by multiple artists credited equally as primary artists, it will count for all of them. Features, writing credits, production credits, or other forms of non-primary contributions to a song will not count. Artist attribution will be determined solely by the artists displayed by Billboard on the relevant chart.

Billboard updates its Hot 100 songs chart each Tuesday (with adjusted release schedules on some holiday weeks), reflecting data collected in the previous week (Friday-Thursday). Each Billboard chart is then titled “Week of (date of the upcoming Saturday)”. The first Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the chart titled “Week of January 17, 2026” for a data collection reference period of January 2-8, 2026. The final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will be the Chart titled “Week of January 9, 2027” for a data collection reference period of December 25-31, 2026.

Updates or revisions made within this market’s timeframe to the #1-ranked song on previously published Billboard Hot 100 charts will be considered; however, such updates will not disqualify a previously published Billboard Hot 100 #1 song from counting. If a Billboard Hot 100 #1 song is revised after initial publication, both the originally published #1 song and the revised #1 song will count for this market. Updates or revisions made after the release of the final Billboard Hot 100 song chart relevant to this market will not be considered.

The resolution source for this market will be the weekly Billboard Hot 100 songs charts published each week at https://www.billboard.com/charts/hot-100/ and through other official Billboard channels.

eventMetadata.context_description: Olivia Rodrigo's "Drop Dead" debuted at No. 1 on the Billboard Hot 100 for the May 2, 2026 chart, securing her among nine artists achieving a 2026 chart-topper—including Ella Langley's record seven-week run on "Choosin' Texas," BTS' "Swim," and Taylor Swift's dual hits—fueled by explosive streaming surges and radio play. This past month's shifts highlight country-pop crossovers and K-pop momentum disrupting traditional pop leads, with Luminate metrics underscoring viral TikTok traction and sales data as key drivers. Trader consensus anticipates summer releases from Drake, Ariana Grande, and Post Malone, leveraging their historical chart dominance and promotional firepower, amid weekly chart updates and potential guild-like precursor buzz from Spotify streams that could crown new frontrunners before year-end.

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the PA-06 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the PA-06 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices Democratic victory at 92.5¢ in Pennsylvania's 6th Congressional District House race, reflecting incumbent Rep. Chrissy Houlahan's commanding position in a Solid Democratic seat with Cook PVI D+6. Houlahan, an Air Force veteran seeking a fifth term, secured 56% in 2024 and larger margins previously against underfunded challengers, buoyed by Chester County's suburban, college-educated voters who have trended reliably blue. With March filing deadlines passed, her May 19 primary faces no serious opposition, while Republican Marty Young enters without national buzz or fundraising edge. Odds hold firm absent a Houlahan scandal, primary upset, or strong national Republican midterm wave tilting battleground Pennsylvania.

eventMetadata.context_description: The open-seat race in New York's 21st Congressional District, rated Solid Republican with an R+10 partisan lean where Donald Trump carried 60% in 2024, drives trader consensus favoring the Republican Party at 66.5% implied probability. Incumbent Elise Stefanik's departure for a gubernatorial bid opened the field, but a competitive GOP primary pits self-funded Anthony Constantino—backed by President Trump's April 21 endorsement and leading early polls—with state GOP-endorsed Assemblyman Robert Smullen, both boasting multimillion-dollar war chests. Democrats face a fragmented primary led by well-funded Blake Gendebien amid lower overall resources, underscoring the uphill battle in this battleground's northern New York terrain ahead of June 23 primaries and the November 3 general election.

markets.question: Will the NYT front-page headlines say "AI" or "Intelligence" this week?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed term is included in a headline on the New York Times front page between May 4 and May 10, 2026. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A headline is defined as the bolded or enlarged text directly preceding each article, previewing the article’s content and typically separated from the article’s text by a black line and byline. The primary headline for each story is the headline for that story with the largest text, typically appearing in bold font and above any other headlines or text for that article.

Sub-headlines, defined as additional bolded or enlarged text not separated from the primary headline by any text, will count, whether they appear before the byline or are partially surrounded by the article text but still adjacent to the primary headline. Pull quotes, however, or any bolded text not adjacent to the primary headline, will not count.

Banner headlines, defined as front-page headlines bordered on the sides only by white space, will count.

Image captions, article text, or any other text that does not constitute a headline, will not qualify.

Any plural or possessive forms of a listed term, as well as variations in capitalization, will count toward the resolution of this market, regardless of context. Other forms of the listed term will not count.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all slang forms, will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

If the listed term appears as part of a compound word, usage of that compound word qualifies, provided the listed term remains a distinct component of the compound. This does not include suffixes, prefixes, alternative tenses, or grammatical variations that alter the root word. (E.g. if the listed term is joy, killjoy qualifies but joyful does not. E.g. if the listed term is sun, sunflower qualifies but sunny does not.)
If the listed term is part of a hyphenated compound, usage of that hyphenated compound qualifies. (E.g. if the listed term is NATO, pro-NATO and anti-NATO qualify.)

The resolution source for this market will be the New York Times daily newspaper, including images of the front page posted daily at: https://nytimes.pressreader.com/the-new-york-times/

markets.question: Will there be no head of state of Venezuela end of 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the individual who officially holds the position of the head of state of Venezuela on Dec 31, 2026 at 12 PM ET.

For the purposes of this market, "officially holds" refers to the individual that was formally appointed, confirmed (if confirmation is required), and sworn in as the head of state of Venezuela or otherwise confirmed by official government information as being the head of state of Venezuela. 

If the Venezuelan government does not clearly state who is the head of state, the market will resolve to the individual who is listed as the Head of State by the UN (see: https://www.un.org/dgacm/en/content/protocol/hshgnfa).  

In the event that more than one official head of state is listed by the Venezuelan  government, this market will resolve to the individual who is listed as having primary status. If no distinction is made, the market will resolve to the individual who first assumed the position. 

If no individual holds the position this market will resolve to “No Head of State”. 

The following do NOT constitute "officially holding" the role: nominated, announced, or designated as the head of state of Venezuela but appointment not yet effective; appointed with an effective date after Dec 31, 2026; previously served as the head of state of Venezuela but term has expired, resigned, or been terminated before Dec 31, 2026; serving as the head of state of Venezuela in a consultant, contractor, or unofficial capacity; named as successor or heir apparent to the head of state of Venezuela but not yet appointed; or holding a different role with oversight of the head of state of Venezuela but not the specified position itself.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the UN recognized government of Venezuela. If the Government of Venezuela does not clearly state who is the head of state, information from the UN and a consensus of credible reporting may be used. 

Note: this market is mutually exclusive.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Nicolás Maduro remaining Venezuela's head of state by end-2026 at 64.4%, despite his January 3 US military capture and ongoing detention in New York on narcoterrorism charges, as chavismo loyalists decry it a temporary "kidnapping" and signal preparations for his potential return amid stalled extradition proceedings. Acting President Delcy Rodríguez, sworn in January 5 and holding power beyond her 90-day constitutional limit, trades at 21.5% amid internal purges of Maduro allies, military reshuffles, and US sanction relief in April enabling oil reforms and direct flights resumption this week. Opposition leader María Corina Machado at 7.5% builds coalition support for her candidacy and year-end return, urging swift elections, though stabilization delays any vote timeline.

markets.question: NK Istra 1961 vs. NK Slaven Belupo: O/U 2.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming Prva Liga game between NK Istra 1961 and NK Slaven Belupo, scheduled for May 4 at 12:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if NK Istra 1961 and NK Slaven Belupo combine to score 3 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 3, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on hnl.hr. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: NK Istra 1961 vs. NK Slaven Belupo - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Prva Liga game, scheduled for May 4 at 12:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: NK Istra 1961 hosts NK Slaven Belupo in a mid-table HNL clash at Stadion Aldo Drosina, with Slaven holding 5th place and Istra 7th after around 30 matches, both chasing European spots or consolidation amid a tight standings battle behind leaders Dinamo Zagreb and Hajduk Split. Istra enters off a 2-1 home win over Vukovar on April 18 and a tough Dinamo Zagreb fixture on April 22, showing decent home form, while Slaven drew 2-2 at Hajduk Split on April 17 before facing Lokomotiva on April 21, reflecting solid away resilience. Head-to-head records are balanced with frequent draws; Istra's Israel Isaac Ayuma remains sidelined by injury, but no other major absences reported in the past 30 days. Late-season motivation and home advantage could sway this evenly matched encounter.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Hong Kong be 21°C on April 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded by the Hong Kong Observatory in degrees Celsius on 30 Apr '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from the Hong Kong Observatory, specifically the "Absolute Daily Max (deg. C)" the specified date once information is finalized in the relevant "Daily Extract", available here: https://www.weather.gov.hk/en/cis/climat.htm

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures in Celsius to one decimal place (eg, 9.1°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Hong Kong Observatory's official measurement confirms the highest temperature on April 30, 2026, reached 25°C or higher, driving trader consensus to a near-certain 99.8% implied probability amid an exceptionally warm April overall. Despite a cooler late-month regime from a northeast monsoon ushering cloudy skies, showers, and a temperature drop starting late on April 29, daytime solar heating under mainly cloudy conditions still elevated readings above the 25°C threshold at the Observatory. This aligns with climatological norms for April, where daily maxima average around 27°C, even during transient cool spells. Scenarios challenging this would require rare data revisions from quality-controlled observations, with market resolution hinging on final HKO verification.

markets.question: Will Southampton achieve promotion from the EFL Championship to the English Premier League for the 2026-27 season?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the listed team achieves promotion from the EFL Championship to the English Premier League (EPL) for the 2026–27 season at the conclusion of the current league season. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Official promotion is defined as a team clinching a place in the English Premier League (EPL) for the 2026–27 season through their final standing in the EFL Championship, via playoff victory, or by any other official league decision recognized by the EFL Championship and the English Premier League (EPL).

If the 2025-26 EFL Championship season is cancelled, postponed after June 12, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, or there are no teams confirmed for promotion within that timeframe, the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

The primary resolution source will be official announcements from the EFL Championship and the English Premier League (EPL). A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Coventry City clinched the EFL Championship title and automatic Premier League promotion with a 4-0 win over Watford on the final day, while Ipswich Town secured second place via a 3-0 victory against QPR amid tense permutations. Hull City's comeback triumph over Norwich City leapfrogged Wrexham into sixth, finalizing the playoff field: Millwall (3rd, 83 pts), Southampton (4th, 80 pts), Middlesbrough (5th, 80 pts), and Hull (6th, 73 pts). Semifinals begin May 8 with Millwall hosting Hull and Southampton hosting Middlesbrough; aggregate winners advance to the May 23 Wembley final for the third EPL spot. Recent momentum, home advantage in first legs, and playoff experience shape trader consensus on the richest game in football.

markets.question: RC Celta de Vigo vs. Elche CF: O/U 4.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming La Liga game between RC Celta de Vigo and Elche CF, scheduled for May 3 at 8:00 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if RC Celta de Vigo and Elche CF combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on laliga.com. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: RC Celta de Vigo vs. Elche CF - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the La Liga game, scheduled for May 3 at 8:00 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Celta Vigo, entrenched in 6th place in La Liga with a potent attack boasting 44 goals, host relegation-battling Elche CF (18th) at Estadio Abanca-Balaídos amid a troubling dip in form, including a 3-0 Europa League first-leg loss to Freiburg last week, three straight defeats, and no clean sheets in their last five outings. Injuries to key defender Carl Starfelt and midfielder Miguel Román—out for the season—expose defensive vulnerabilities, while Elche arrives with momentum from a 1-0 victory over Valencia but hampered by absences like Marc Aguado and Grady Diangana, plus dismal away form with zero wins on the road. Head-to-head tilts slightly to Celta (7-5-3), amplifying home advantage in this survival scrap.

markets.question: Will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit (HIGH) $304 in May?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if, at any point during May 2026, any 1-minute candle for Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) has a final "High" price equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Only prices achieved during the regular trading hours of the primary exchange on which the listed security trades (typically 9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET) will be considered. Prices occurring during pre-market or after-hours trading will not qualify.

Prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

In the event of a stock split, reverse stock split, or similar corporate action affecting the listed company during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on split-adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The target price will be adjusted proportionally to reflect any stock splits. Resolution will be based on the historical price data as shown on Pyth after any adjustments have been applied.

The resolution source for this market is Pyth — specifically, the Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) "High" prices available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD, with the chart settings configured for 1-minute candles.

Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter. Any timestamp within the listed market time frame may be used to view the relevant candle data (e.g., https://pythdata.app/explore/Equity.US.AMZN%2FUSD?t=1773432000)

If the relevant Pyth data is unavailable due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption that prevents verification of the required 1-minute candle data, the official daily high price published by the primary exchange on which the listed security trades will be used to determine whether the listed price was reached during the applicable trading session.

events.title: What will Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) hit in May 2026?

eventMetadata.context_description: Amazon.com, Inc. (AMZN) shares trade near $268 following a robust Q1 2026 earnings release on April 29, where net sales surged 17% year-over-year to $181.5 billion and operating income climbed to $23.9 billion. Amazon Web Services (AWS) led with 28% revenue growth to $37.6 billion—its fastest pace in 15 quarters—fueled by accelerating AI infrastructure demand and enterprise adoption, bolstering trader confidence in sustained cloud dominance. At a forward P/E of around 32x, the stock reflects optimism amid e-commerce recovery and advertising gains, with analyst consensus price targets averaging $310, implying 15% upside. Key May watchpoints include macroeconomic data like May nonfarm payrolls and consumer spending indicators, ahead of Q2 earnings in late July.
